title: Auto-Categorisation on Import
summary: Automatically assign a category to imported transactions based on description matching. Matching strategy and UX approach still open.
outcome_note: Shipped via AutoAllocationSuggester (app/services/auto_allocation_suggester.rb) + POST /budgets/:id/auto_allocate. Two-pass description match (exact + substring) against previous budget; ambiguity returns nil. Suggest-and-confirm UX in ImportPage.vue (Preview shows exact-✓/contains-? indicators, clearable before Import). Tested. Not implemented (not committed to in idea): fuzzy/learned/ML matching, payee/amount-based matching.

title: Resilient / Format-Agnostic Transaction Import
summary: Make the import pipeline durable against bank export format changes. Minimum: handle small variations without code fixes. Ambitious: fully format-agnostic importer.

title: Google Auth Migration
summary: Replace username/password auth with Google OAuth and eventually decommission password-based login entirely.
outcome_note: Shipped to prod v87 (2026-06-19): Google OAuth as primary sign-in, backend token swap + frontend Authorization: Bearer, sessions + last_active_at migrations applied. Parallel-auth approach delivered. Remaining devise-removal cleanup tracked as separate tasks: remove-password-based-auth-after-google-oauth-migration, drop-legacy-devise-auth-columns-post-prod-soak, restore-cypress-e2e-auth-for-google-only-bearer-token-auth.

title: Backend observability (OpenTelemetry)
summary: Instrument Rails (and optionally Vue) with OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing and performance visibility, no vendor lock-in. Scoped down from the original analytics+observability idea — GA / user-keyed product analytics retired (only two users, never pays off).

title: Everycent MCP Server
summary: Build an MCP server as the reporting/querying layer. Users ask natural language questions via any MCP client. Possible alternative transaction ingestion path. Likely built in Go.
outcome_note: Merged into natural-language-querying-nlq-for-everycent as an architectural component (thin translation layer above Rails /api/mcp/ endpoints). No longer tracked as a separate idea.

title: Periodic refactoring process and /refactor skill
summary: Before each feature migration, run a structured refactoring pass: inventory shared patterns, identify extraction candidates, propose changes, apply and test. Process needs to be tried 2-3 times manually before extracting into a /refactor skill. Too vague to scope yet — try the process out first and see what works and doesn't.
outcome_note: Superseded by automate-the-healthcheck-loop-into-a-self-cleaning-system. Unique content (structured refactoring-pass recipe + extract-a-/refactor-skill-after-manual-runs angle) folded into that idea on 2026-06-21.

title: Inline code review tooling (no MR)
summary: When doing code cleanup without a PR, it would be useful to annotate lines of code directly like a typical code review. Either explore existing tooling or eventually build a code review extension for this purpose.
outcome_note: Deferred 2026-06-21. Largely obviated by cmux's diff viewer (comment-on-lines), which covers the want while using cmux. Built-in local-review skill exists but is clunky; /code-review + senior-code-reviewer don't give line-level annotation UX. NEXT-REVIEW RULE: if nothing has changed by the next deferred-ideas review, abandon.

title: Everycent mobile app
summary: Build a mobile app for everycent as a separate codebase/project. Key challenge: getting transactions from a bank's mobile app/website into everycent. On desktop, copy-paste works. Need to ideate on viable options for mobile transaction import.
outcome_note: Direction settled, ideation done (offline-sync resolved 2026-04-06). Phase 1 (mobile web) already spawning tasks: mobile-layout-setup-screens, mobile chevron a11y, mobile summary-grid alignment, mobile sink-fund inline validation, mobile calculator column. Prereq google-auth-migration now done. Dedup carved out as transaction-fingerprinting-for-dedup task. Remaining work = phased execution.

title: Domus task commit workflow
summary: Domus task and idea files sit in .domus/ and are tracked by git, but there's no clear convention for when to commit them. They can drift uncommitted for long periods. Need to figure out the right trigger points — e.g. end of session, when a task transitions to done, when a spec is finalised — and whether they should be batched with related code commits or kept separate.
outcome_note: Resolved by established convention (2026-06-21). CLAUDE.md Housekeeping Commits: .domus/ + CLAUDE.md + knowledge-base go direct to master; knowledge-base gets own commits; always stage index files (tasks.jsonl, ideas.jsonl) with .domus changes. Project-memory feedback domus-commits-generic-save: treat .domus commits as generic low-ceremony saves, bundle freely, don't fuss over granularity. Landed answer: .domus = generic saves direct to master batched with indexes; code-completing status changes ride along; knowledge-base kept separate.

title: Doctor Prototype: Full Vue 3 Codebase Review
summary: Use a comprehensive webclientv4 code review as the test case for prototyping the Domus Doctor role — capture the process to make it reproducible.

title: Natural Language Querying (NLQ) for Everycent
summary: Enable users to query their finance data in plain English using LLM + pgvector + SQL. Requires new infrastructure (pgvector, embeddings, MCP server) but unlocks powerful ad-hoc analysis.
outcome_note: Phases 1 & 2 shipped (~12 MCP tools incl. analyze_overspending; Vue chat UI; agent loop). As-built architecture DIVERGED from plan: frontend-driven (Ollama in browser, agent loop in chatAgent.ts, Rails = stateless /mcp/* endpoints, no backend agent) — coupled to free-local-model decision. Open: phase 3 pgvector embeddings (not built), production-hosting direction (reopened). Annotated idea with Status/As-Built section flagging stale Architecture section. Spawning many live tasks.

title: Payee Name Backfill for Historical Transactions
summary: Retroactively match historical transaction descriptions to known payee names using fuzzy/semantic matching with human review. Improves embedding quality for NLQ and general data cleanliness.
outcome_note: Implemented via a DIFFERENT approach than this idea proposed. Idea proposed EMBEDDINGS (embed known payees + historical descriptions, nearest-neighbour match, confidence thresholds, human review) — tethered to NLQ phase-3 embeddings which were never built. ACTUAL implementation is DESCRIPTION-BASED DETECTION: PayeeBackfill service (app/services/payee_backfill.rb) + lib/tasks/payee_backfill.rake, using PayeeNameExtractor per-format extractor classes. Reusable parts (PayeeNameExtractor + import-time payee/transfer detection) stay; one-shot backfill machinery removed after prod run via contingent cleanup task remove-payee-backfill-rake-task-and-payeebackfill-service-after-successful-run. NOTE: confirm prod hh96 backfill run + acceptance is done (cleanup task gates on it).

title: Hunter: LLM-Powered Bug Reporting & Autonomous Fix Pipeline
summary: A new domus role (hunter) that conducts diagnostic interviews with non-dev users via an in-app chat interface, investigates using source code, server logs, and DB queries, then hands off a well-understood bug to the taskmaster for autonomous fix dispatch.
outcome_note: Scoped — partially underway and a definite to-do. Slice 1 (bug-reporting chat interface, front end of Hunter) in flight on parked add-bug-reporting-to-the-nlq-chat branch (everycent-bug-capture worktree, text-only), blocked on chat-transcript-capture task cluster. Broader autonomous-fix pipeline (error_logs capture, deployed-source access, GitHub issue handoff, taskmaster→worker→merge loop) still exploratory.

title: Redesign EverycentApp UI using Google Stitch
summary: After the Vue 3 migration completes, use Google Stitch to generate a fresh UI design — a visual refresh driven by AI-generated high-fidelity screens.

title: Dependency-ordered task display in domus overview
summary: Display tasks in topological (dependency) order within each status category in domus task overview, replacing the separate Blocked section. The ordering itself communicates priority — blocked tasks just appear later in the list.
outcome_note: Moved to its proper home — the domus project. This was a domus-CLI display concern captured in everycent's backlog only incidentally. Folded into domus idea 'improve-task-overview-to-give-better-pipeline-progress-insight' (the topological-ordering-within-status-groups proposal + the 'topo ordering replaces the Blocked bucket for a solo dev' argument + its open questions). Note: current domus overview already shows dependency glyphs and dropped the standalone Blocked section, so may be partially done — verify in domus.

title: Naming convention for bridge composables vs pure stores
summary: useNotifications wraps PrimeVue toast - need a way to distinguish at a glance which composables are safe to use as real stores in tests vs which need mocking because they bridge external services.

title: Extract buildSettingsStore and similar store factories for test setup
summary: After mock-API migration lands, check if settingsStore/headingStore setup is repeated across page specs. If the 2-line pattern (mock API + flushPromises) appears 3+ times, extract a factory. settingsStore is special — router guard pre-loads it, so page tests might not need to set it up themselves.
outcome_note: Folded into task migrate-all-spects-files-to-use-test-factories as a follow-up note. Not implemented (no buildSettingsStore/store-setup factory exists), but precondition not met: settingsStore setup appears only ~3-4x in 4 inconsistent styles — below a clean rule-of-three bar. The special case the idea predicted is confirmed: router beforeEach guard pre-loads settingsStore (src/router/index.ts:181), so router-navigated specs don't need inline setup. Re-evaluate during/after the spec migration; belongs with that work, not as a standalone idea.

title: Clean up domus task list display and status sync
outcome_note: Empty stub (title only, zero captured content). Domus-tool concern, not everycent. Both implied threads already owned elsewhere: 'task list display' → domus idea improve-task-overview-to-give-better-pipeline-progress-insight; 'status sync' (task-index ↔ task-file consistency) → task doctor-skill-task-index-consistency-check. Nothing to preserve or move.

title: Auto-generate TypeScript types from Rails API
summary: Derive frontend TypeScript type definitions from the Rails API's actual serializer output, eliminating hand-maintained parallel type definitions and guaranteeing FE/BE type alignment.

title: Decide auth migration direction off devise_token_auth
summary: devise_token_auth is dormant and blocks Rails 8; decide whether to keep password login (keep devise + devise-jwt) or go Google-only (drop devise, owned opaque bearer tokens). The fork hinges on the password-login decision.
outcome_note: Decision made AND executed. Decided 2026-06-06: go Google-only, drop devise + devise_token_auth, owned opaque bearer-token scheme (sessions table, SHA256 digest, not JWT), auth-first sequencing. Scoped into task migrate-to-google-only-auth-with-owned-opaque-tokens; SHIPPED to prod v87 (2026-06-19): backend token swap, frontend Authorization: Bearer, sessions + last_active_at migrations, and the Rails 8.1 + Ruby 3.4 upgrade this was blocking. Remaining = cleanup tasks (remove-password-based-auth, drop-legacy-devise-auth-columns, restore-cypress-e2e-auth) + carried break-glass-fallback question — all task-level.

title: Filter/exclude certain allocation categories from NLQ tools
summary: User foresees wanting to hide or filter out certain allocation categories in the NLQ layer (e.g. from list_categories and downstream tools) — likely the bookkeeping/structural categories that aren't meaningful spending. CategoryList query object (app/models/mcp/category_list.rb) was deliberately given a home now so this filter has somewhere to land. Decide: filter by budget_role, an explicit exclude-list, or a per-category flag; and whether it's a tool param or a fixed default.
outcome_note: Deferred 2026-06-21 — core concern largely mitigated, list-level filter is a nice-to-have. As-built: analysis tools already exclude structural/bookkeeping categories via shared Mcp::SpendingScope (budget_role='spending' + placeholder/manual-adjustment/B-F exclusions), so structural categories don't pollute NLQ analysis results. CategoryList (list_categories tool) still returns ALL categories BUT now exposes budget_role per category, so the LLM can reason about structural vs spending itself. Remaining unbuilt piece = filtering list_categories output outright, which may be unnecessary given the above. REVISIT if NLQ actually trips over structural categories in practice.

title: Capture NLQ chat transcripts for later analysis/debugging
summary: Persist NLQ chat transcripts (turns, tool calls, tool results, token data) so the agent's behaviour can be debugged after the fact. Nothing chat-related is persisted today. Surfaced while designing the bug-reporting feature; the auto-attach-transcript-to-bug seam links the two.
outcome_note: Implemented — core capture + purge built (2026-06). Currently on ready-for-master (5 ahead of master), being merged in a separate session. Commits: 252b79064 backend tables+recorder+conversation_turns endpoint; 9516a93e9 harden (reject empty steps, return steps_created); d9bc3a3ec frontend capture+flush to /mcp/conversation_turns; 333de6bb6 purge rake task (180d retention, no scheduling); 9f213378d rake desc autoload fix. Covers the 3 core cluster tasks (backend / frontend / purge). Two optional follow-on tasks remain: make-chat-transcript-retention-window-configurable-from-settings-ui, schedule-the-chat-transcript-purge-job (purge built 'no scheduling' by design). Unblocks Hunter Slice 1 (add-bug-reporting-to-the-nlq-chat) once merged.

title: Automate the healthcheck loop into a self-cleaning system
summary: Today healthcheck is a me-in-the-loop process (audits + fixes, dependency updates, behaviour-preserving refactors). Explore automating the whole loop so the system self-cleans and surfaces only problems/decisions to Kion. This is background orchestration operating ON the repo — closer to a Claude Code / domus-harness / scheduled-agent shape than to the in-app NLQ chat agent. Distinct from chat-agent work and does not bear on the NLQ agent hosting decision. Direction unknown — exploratory.

title: CSV import for credit cards
summary: Switch credit-card imports from manual entry to CSV import from Mijn ICS (ABN AMRO cards have no CAMT; CSV is the only structured export). Feasibility hinges on a stable per-transaction ID for dedup and a non-cumbersome CSV download.

title: First-class conversations table (parent of conversation_turns)
summary: Whether to add a first-class `conversations` model/table as the parent of `conversation_turns`, completing the conversation → turns → steps hierarchy. Deferred — revisit when a conversation-level fact or behaviour actually appears.

title: Verifier-Gated Autopilot Harness for Autonomous Dev Loops
summary: A reusable harness that takes a goal + worktree and drives an AI loop to a machine-checkable 'stable, working' conclusion, gated by an external un-fakeable verifier rather than the agent's self-assessment. The human stays permanently accountable, but the trust boundary widens over time as their review feedback is compiled into new deterministic checks.
